subject: Re: Celia's Alternatives
body:
err on saftey first... if sweden is easier   send her
home today

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> I have reviewed Celia's docs and my immigration
> materials and have come up  
> with the following alternatives:
>  
> 1.    Enter USA on Visa Waiver Program through
> Canada  or Sweden
> 2.    Enter USA on Tourist Visa through Canada or 
> Sweden
> 3.    Enter USA on existing H-1B notice of approval 
> through Sweden
> 4.    Enter USA on a revised H-1B notice of approval
>  which we apply for on 
> an expedited basis.
>  
> Note:    Celia's driver's license is a foreign 
> license.
>             Celia  currently is in possession of her
> passport and the expired 
> H-1B visa has been  stamped cancelled.
>             Celia  was denied a visa only for
> insufficient documentation to 
> show that there is  unused H-1B time left within the
> 6 year limit or that Celia.
>             Tibby  claims that Canadian consular
> officer was incorrect in not 
> giving Celia credit  for time outside of US plus
> time in US on Visa Waiver, 
> but since grant of visa  is always at discretion of
> consular officer, nothing 
> can be done .  Tibby  claims best bet is to go to
> Sweden and enter through 
> Sweden..
>             Tibby  says that he does not know what
> is in the consulate 
> computer system about what  transpired and does not
> know what Sweden consulate 
> officer will do regarding  existing H-1B notice of
> approval, but thinks it would be 
> worth a try
>             Tibby  wants to apply for amended status
> to obtain an H-1B notice 
> of approval for  shorter period of time (4-6 months)
> and wants to use this 
> for a visa to be  obtained in Sweden
>             I  have asked Tibby to speak with the
> Canadian attorney to 
> determine likelihood of  admission to the US
> throughCanada based on Visa Waiver or 
> Tourist Visa.
>  
> Visa Waiver requires departure and re-entry after
> each 90 day period.
>             Tourist  Visa can be extended while in
> US.  
> 


__________________________________________________

body_html:
err on saftey first... if sweden is easier send her
home today

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

I have reviewed Celia's docs and my immigration
materials and have come up
with the following alternatives:

  1. Enter USA on Visa Waiver Program through

Canada or Sweden
2. Enter USA on Tourist Visa through Canada or
Sweden
3. Enter USA on existing H-1B notice of approval
through Sweden
4. Enter USA on a revised H-1B notice of approval
which we apply for on
an expedited basis.

Note: Celia's driver's license is a foreign
license.
Celia currently is in possession of her
passport and the expired
H-1B visa has been stamped cancelled.
Celia was denied a visa only for
insufficient documentation to
show that there is unused H-1B time left within the
6 year limit or that Celia.
Tibby claims that Canadian consular
officer was incorrect in not
giving Celia credit for time outside of US plus
time in US on Visa Waiver,
but since grant of visa is always at discretion of
consular officer, nothing
can be done . Tibby claims best bet is to go to
Sweden and enter through
Sweden..
Tibby says that he does not know what
is in the consulate
computer system about what transpired and does not
know what Sweden consulate
officer will do regarding existing H-1B notice of
approval, but thinks it would be
worth a try
Tibby wants to apply for amended status
to obtain an H-1B notice
of approval for shorter period of time (4-6 months)
and wants to use this
for a visa to be obtained in Sweden
I have asked Tibby to speak with the
Canadian attorney to
determine likelihood of admission to the US
throughCanada based on Visa Waiver or
Tourist Visa.

Visa Waiver requires departure and re-entry after
each 90 day period.
Tourist Visa can be extended while in
US.

Do You Yahoo!?

http://mail.yahoo.com

body:
first choice is to get her back quickly   even for a 
short whil ,, so she can finish school...  then we can
talk about it
